qa_pairs.question: How can I run Ansys programs in graphical mode?
qa_pairs.answer: To run Ansys programs in graphical mode, you need to click on one of the provided OnDemand or Jupyterhub desktop links for the respective cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the OnDemand desktop link for NIBI?
qa_pairs.answer: The OnDemand desktop link for NIBI is `https://ondemand.sharcnet.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Jupyterhub desktop link for FIR?
qa_pairs.answer: The Jupyterhub desktop link for FIR is `https://jupyterhub.fir.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Jupyterhub desktop link for RORQUAL?
qa_pairs.answer: The Jupyterhub desktop link for RORQUAL is `https://jupyterhub.rorqual.alliancecan.ca/`.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required after accessing an OnDemand or Jupyterhub desktop link?
qa_pairs.answer: After accessing the link, a job submission web page will appear in your browser. You must configure the resources required for your interactive desktop session and then click 'Launch' or 'Start'. Ensure you specify a GPU resource if accelerated graphics or computations will be conducted within your desktop session.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load an Ansys module once my desktop session starts in graphical mode?
qa_pairs.answer: If you started a Jupyter Lab-powered desktop, you can load an Ansys module by clicking the left-hand menu. If you started an OnDemand desktop, you must manually type `module load ansys/version` on the command line.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find Slurm scripts for AnsysEDT?
qa_pairs.answer: Slurm scripts for using AnsysEDT are provided in a separate wiki page, accessible here: [https://docs.alliancecan.ca/wiki/AnsysEDT].
